TC1.Test Description: Verifies that configuring PA0 as output and writing 1 then 0 to the GPIO output data register bit 0 is reflected by subsequent reads of the same register bit.
TC1.Test Steps / Procedure:
1) Enable GPIOA clock: RCC_AHB1ENR |= (1 << 0).
2) Configure PA0 as output: GPIOA_MODER &= ~(3U << 0); then GPIOA_MODER |= (1U << 0).
3) Drive PA0 high: GPIOA_ODR |= (1 << 0).
4) Read-back check for high: if (!(GPIOA_ODR & (1 << 0))) -> FAIL.
5) Drive PA0 low: GPIOA_ODR &= ~(1 << 0).
6) Read-back check for low: if (GPIOA_ODR & (1 << 0)) -> FAIL.
7) On success -> PASS().
TC1.Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- After setting GPIOA_ODR bit 0, condition (GPIOA_ODR & (1 << 0)) must be non-zero; else FAIL("FAIL: GPIO WRITE HIGH
").
- After clearing GPIOA_ODR bit 0, condition (GPIOA_ODR & (1 << 0)) must be zero; else FAIL("FAIL: GPIO WRITE LOW
").
- PASS() prints "GPIO REG WR/RD TEST PASSED
" when both checks succeed.
TC1.Gap Analysis:
- Does not verify actual pad state via input register or external observation; only reads back the ODR register.
- Only bit 0 is tested; no coverage for other GPIO bits or alternate modes.
- No explicit verification that GPIOA_MODER update took effect beyond successful ODR read-back.

TC2.Feature: EXTI falling-edge trigger enable on alternate pads
TC2.Test Description: Enables EXTI falling-edge trigger for lines 0, 2, 4, and 6 and verifies each corresponding bit is set in EXTI_FTSR.
TC2.Test Steps / Procedure:
1) Define target EXTI lines: pins = {0, 2, 4, 6}.
2) For each pin in pins: set falling trigger enable: EXTI_FTSR |= (1 << pin).
3) Read-back each write: if (!(EXTI_FTSR & (1 << pin))) -> FAIL.
4) On success -> PASS().
TC2.Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- For each selected line, after write, (EXTI_FTSR & (1 << pin)) must be non-zero; otherwise FAIL("FAIL: NEDGE ALT PAD\n").
- PASS() prints "GPIO NEDGE ALT PADS TEST PASSED\n" when all checks succeed.
TC2.Gap Analysis:
- Does not enable EXTI interrupt mask (IMR) or map GPIO ports; only verifies configuration bits in FTSR.
- Does not verify actual falling-edge detection or pending flag/ISR behavior.

TC3.Feature: GPIO negative-edge interrupt enable via EXTI
TC3.Test Description: Configures PA1 as the EXTI1 source, enables EXTI interrupt mask and falling-edge trigger for line 1, and verifies that the falling-edge trigger bit is set.
TC3.Test Steps / Procedure:
1) Enable peripheral clocks: RCC_AHB1ENR |= (1 << 0); RCC_APB2ENR |= (1 << 14).
2) Configure PA1 as input: GPIOA_MODER &= ~(3U << (1 * 2)).
3) Map PA1 to EXTI1: SYSCFG_EXTICR1 &= ~(0xF << 4).
4) Enable EXTI line: EXTI_IMR |= (1 << 1).
5) Enable falling-edge trigger: EXTI_FTSR |= (1 << 1).
6) Verify configuration: if (!(EXTI_FTSR & (1 << 1))) -> FAIL.
7) On success -> PASS().
TC3.Impacted Registers: RCC_AHB1ENR, RCC_APB2ENR, GPIOA_MODER, SYSCFG_EXTICR1, EXTI_IMR, EXTI_FTSR
TC3.Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- After configuration, (EXTI_FTSR & (1 << 1)) must be non-zero; else FAIL("FAIL: NEG EDGE NOT ENABLED\n").
- PASS() prints "GPIO NEG EDGE INTR TEST PASSED\n" when the check succeeds.
TC3.Gap Analysis:
- Does not confirm EXTI_IMR bit state via check.
- No stimulus to generate a falling edge; no pending flag (PR) or ISR verification.
- No deconfiguration or cleanup sequencing validated.

TC4.Feature: EXTI rising-edge trigger enable on all pads
TC4.Test Description: Enables EXTI rising-edge trigger for lines 0 through 7 and verifies each corresponding bit is set in EXTI_RTSR.
TC4.Test Steps / Procedure:
1) Iterate lines i = 0..7.
2) For each i: EXTI_RTSR |= (1 << i).
3) Read-back each write: if (!(EXTI_RTSR & (1 << i))) -> FAIL.
4) On success -> PASS().
TC4.Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- For each line 0..7, after write, (EXTI_RTSR & (1 << i)) must be non-zero; otherwise FAIL("FAIL: PEDGE ALL PADS\n").
- PASS() prints "GPIO PEDGE ALL PADS TEST PASSED\n" when all checks succeed.
TC4.Gap Analysis:
- Does not configure GPIO-to-EXTI mapping or enable interrupt mask (IMR).
- Does not validate actual rising-edge detection or interrupt handling.
- Only covers EXTI lines 0..7; lines beyond 7 are not exercised.

TC5.Feature: EXTI rising-edge trigger enable on alternate pads
TC5.Test Description: Enables EXTI rising-edge trigger for lines 0, 2, 4, and 6 and verifies each corresponding bit is set in EXTI_RTSR.
TC5.Test Steps / Procedure:
1) Define target EXTI lines: pins = {0, 2, 4, 6}.
2) For each pin: EXTI_RTSR |= (1 << pin).
3) Read-back each write: if (!(EXTI_RTSR & (1 << pin))) -> FAIL.
4) On success -> PASS().
TC5.Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- For each selected line, after write, (EXTI_RTSR & (1 << pin)) must be non-zero; otherwise FAIL("FAIL: PEDGE ALT PAD\n").
- PASS() prints "GPIO PEDGE ALT PADS TEST PASSED\n" when all checks succeed.
TC5.Gap Analysis:
- Does not configure GPIO-to-EXTI mapping or enable interrupt mask (IMR).
- Does not validate actual rising-edge detection or ISR behavior.
